title: isolation_rr_si
excerpt: Here’s a question: when you choose Repeatable Read isolation level in MySQL, does it actually give you Snapshot Isolation semantics instead? How different are they and why does it matter?
content_html:
Repeatable Read vs Snapshot Isolation

Here’s a question: when you choose Repeatable Read isolation level in MySQL, does it actually give you Snapshot Isolation semantics instead? How different are they and why does it matter?

Theory

ANSI Standard Isolation Levels

PostgreSQL, MySQL, SAP, and many other databases implement the four common isolation levels which are defined in SQL-92 based on anomaly protection:

Isolation Summary A1 A2 A3
READ UNCOMMITTED weakest Yes Yes Yes
READ COMMITTED basic atomicity no Yes Yes
REPEATABLE READ basic isolation no no Yes
SERIALIZABLE strongest no no no

Anomaly A1 is “dirty reads” (T1 sees the data T2 wrote before it commits, and indeed T1 might even see it if T2 is rolled back).

Anomaly A2 is “inconsistent reads” (T1 reads a row, T2 does something to it and commits, and T1 reads it again but gets a different value).

Anomaly A3 is “phantoms” (T1 reads some rows via a select predicate, T2 changes the set of rows matching that predicate, e.g. by adding a new row, and commits, and T1 reads again but this time the select results are slightly different due to the actions of T2, so they still aren’t perfectly isolated).

Theoretical Relation to Locking

A bit of an oversimplification, but in traditional theory, these levels were implemented by different locking regimes for phantom prevention, read sets, and write sets, e.g. none, short (only during the operation), or long (hold till the end of the transaction):

Isolation Phantom Locks Read Locks Write Locks
READ UNCOMMITTED no no long
READ COMMITTED no short long
REPEATABLE READ short long long
SERIALIZABLE long long long

Note that the phantom prevention locks can take many forms, e.g. predicate locks, precision locks, gap locks, range locks, index locks, etc, but the basic goal is to make sure the matched set of a predicate does not vary, e.g. between repeated SELECTs in a transaction.

But in Reality

Modern databases don’t necessarily provide exactly these isolation semantics or implement them using the above simple locking regimes.

MVCC systems that can read at a specific timestamp are such a convenient tool and intuitive concept, they are widely used to achieve read set isolation and phantom prevention with less locking, higher performance, and higher concurrency. The practice is now quite prevalent.

Thus, PostgreSQL uses snapshot isolation internally, even in its default isolation level of Read Committed. MySQL also acquires a read snapshot as of the first select in a transaction, and uses the same snapshot during subsequent selects, in its default isolation level of Repeatable Read.

According to A Critique of ANSI SQL Isolation Levels (SIGMOD 1995) by Berenson, Bernstein, Gray et al, the ANSI standard isolation levels suffer from a number of shortcomings, such as failure to address “write skew” anomalies (e.g. the history sequence T1:read[x], T2:read[y], T1:write[y], T2:write[x], T1:commit, T2:commit, which clearly contains a non-serializable overlap between T1 and T2).

The paper Generalized Isolation Level Definitions (ICDE 2000) by Adya, Liskov, et al further elaborates on the theoretical shortcomings of the ANSI standard, and provides more abstract, general, and flexible definitions.

A careful review of the literature shows that Snapshot Isolation (SI) is very similar to Repeatable Read (RR), but they aren’t exactly the same. They are both stronger than Read Committed but weaker than Serializable. The best summary of their difference is that SI allows write skew but blocks phantoms, whereas RR blocks write skew but allows phantoms.

Microsoft extends SQL to distinguish between RR and SI.

Infamously, one large database vender (rhymes with Auracle) sold SI to their customers as Serializable.

Recent versions of PostgreSQL provide an improvement on SI called Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI). For the sake of this document, we will borrow a great example of write skew anomalies from the paper Serializable Snapshot Isolation in PostgreSQL (VLDB 2012) by Dan Ports et al.

Let’s Experiment

MySQL

Installing

As root, quickie unsafe install:

# dnf install -y community-mysql-server
# systemctl start mysqld

Creating a Test Database

# mysql -u root -e 'CREATE DATABASE test'

Connecting to Command Line Prompt

Connect to the insecure database like so:

# mysql -u root -D test

Selecting Isolation Level

InnoDB defaults to RR, but we’ll specify it manually just to be sure.

mysql> SET SESSION TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;

Write Skew Test Setup

MySQL will likely default to InnoDB, but we’ll specify it just to be sure.

CREATE TABLE doctor (name VARCHAR(20), oncall BOOLEAN) ENGINE=InnoDB;
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Andy', TRUE);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Brad', TRUE);
EXIT

PostgreSQL

Installing

As root:

# dnf install -y https://download.postgresql.org/pub/repos/yum/reporpms/F-37-x86_64/pgdg-fedora-repo-latest.noarch.rpm
# dnf install -y postgresql14-server postgresql14
# /usr/pgsql-14/bin/postgresql-14-setup initdb
# systemctl start postgresql-14

Creating a Test Database

# su -c 'createdb test' - postgres

Connecting to Command Line Prompt

# su -c 'psql -d test' - postgres

Selecting Isolation Level

test=# SET SESSION CHARACTERISTICS AS TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;

Write Skew Test Setup

Looks just like with MySQL, except, instead of ENGINE=InnoDB we would say USING someAccessMethod if we wanted to override the default internal table access method.

CREATE TABLE doctor (name VARCHAR(20), oncall BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Andy', TRUE);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Brad', TRUE);
EXIT

The Actual Write Skew Test

Happily, one version suffices for both MySQL and PostgreSQL.

Terminal 1: Andy is checking whether it’s safe for him to take some time off:

BEGIN;
SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE AND name<>'Andy';
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Brad |
+------+

Good. There’s another doctor on call. Speaking of which…

Terminal 2: Brad, the other doctor, has the same plan:

BEGIN;
SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE AND name<>'Brad';
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Andy |
+------+

UPDATE doctor SET oncall=FALSE WHERE name='Brad';
COMMIT;

Cool, Brad is all set.

Back at terminal 1: Andy thinks “quick let’s get in before anything changes”:

UPDATE doctor SET oncall=FALSE WHERE name='Andy';

So far so good. Andy is a careful boy and takes a moment to double check before he commits:

SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE;
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Brad |
+------+

COMMIT;

The latter SELECT result seems to make sense–roughly speaking RR says we see a consistent view of the world, untainted by changes wrought by any other transaction… a stable read set, except we do see changes wrought by our own transaction (i.e. Andy is no longer on call).

Excellent! All’s well that ends well. Andy’s name is not in the result set, because he’s taking time off, but there is at least one name in the result set. Andy reflects on how awesome SQL is.

However… it didn’t actually end well. Imagine Andy’s surpise when Charlie shows him this:

SELECT * FROM doctor;
+------+--------+
| name | oncall |
+------+--------+
| Brad |      0 |
| Andy |      0 |
+------+--------+

WHAT!!!!! How did this happen?

Epilogue

Andy was bitten by the infamous write skew anomaly, which is not allowed under RR, but is allowed under SI. By the way, this happens in both MySQL RR and PostgreSQL RR.

Simply put, Andy and Brad were operating from the same snapshotted read view, against which their writes were individually valid, but the two transactions overlap in a non-serializable way, and the combination of overlapping writes lead to an unacceptable outcome–there is nobody oncall. Write skew can ruin your day. Phantom protection seems fine in this example though–which is again consistent with SI.

What does this tell us about MySQL and PostgreSQL Repeatable Read isolation? It looks technically more like Snapshot Isolation based on these experiments, though it’s probably not purely either one. Perhaps the developers were reticent to depart from the ANSI standard otherwise they would call it Snapshot.

Minor Plot Twist

It’s not exactly an unexpected plot twist, but it turns out RR in MySQL is weirder than you think. It is not really SI either, if you consider the lost updates anomaly, which should not be allowed in either RR or SI.

The aforementioned critique of ANSI isolation levels seems convinced that lost updates are not allowed in RR, and notes that “… the anomaly P4 is useful in distinguishing isolation levels intermediate in strength between READ COMMITTED and REPEATABLE READ.” In other words, RC may exhibit lost updates, but RR does not.

And yet, according to the following test, in MySQL, lost updates happen even in RR. On the other hand, the same test in PostgreSQL does not exhibit the lost update anomaly in RR, though it does in RC, which conforms better to what the literature says for these isolation levels.

Lost Update Test Setup

We just need one row for this…

CREATE TABLE account (id INT, cash INT);
INSERT INTO account (id, cash) VALUES (1, 100);

Lost Update Test

Terminal 1 (seeking to deposit $20):

BEGIN;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  100 |
+------+

While this is going on, in terminal 2 (seeking to deposit $30):

BEGIN;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  100 |
+------+

UPDATE account SET cash=130 WHERE id=1;
COMMIT;

Back to terminal 1, which saw a balance of $100:

UPDATE account SET cash=120 WHERE id=1;
COMMIT;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  120 |
+------+

This is bad, it means the $30 deposit was overwritten. From the user’s perspective, either the total should be $150, or one of the transactions should have failed. This is a thing that can happen to you in RR isolation level on MySQL.

Some Advice re Lost Update

Although Serializable isolation level would prevented this problem in MySQL, if you are doing this type of transaction pattern, consider making the database aware of your intent by using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE.

Summary

People don’t always define terms in the same way (even when they think they do), and practice often diverges from theory. This is especially so in the world of database isolation levels.

The article Comprehensive Understanding of Transaction Isolation Levels has more details comparing the nuances of some popular implementations.

sentences.en: Repeatable Read vs Snapshot Isolation
sentences.zh: 可重复读取与快照隔离

blocks.html_en: Here’s a question: when you choose Repeatable Read isolation level in MySQL, does it actually give you Snapshot Isolation semantics instead? How different are they and why does it matter?

sentences.en: Here’s a question: when you choose Repeatable Read isolation level in MySQL, does it actually give you Snapshot Isolation semantics instead?
sentences.zh: 这里有一个问题：当你在 MySQL 中选择可重复读隔离级别时，它实际上会给你快照隔离语义吗？

sentences.en: How different are they and why does it matter?
sentences.zh: 它们有何不同？为什么如此重要？

sentences.en: Theory
sentences.zh: 理论

sentences.en: ANSI Standard Isolation Levels
sentences.zh: ANSI 标准隔离级别

blocks.html_en: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SAP, and many other databases implement the four common isolation levels which are defined in SQL-92 based on anomaly protection:

sentences.en: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SAP, and many other databases implement the four common isolation levels which are defined in SQL-92 based on anomaly protection:
sentences.zh: PostgreSQL、MySQL、SAP 和许多其他数据库基于异常保护实现了 SQL-92 中定义的四种常见隔离级别：

sentences.en: Isolation
sentences.zh: 隔离

sentences.en: Summary
sentences.zh: 概括

sentences.en: READ UNCOMMITTED
sentences.zh: 未提交的阅读

sentences.en: weakest
sentences.zh: 最弱的

sentences.en: READ COMMITTED
sentences.zh: 已提交读

sentences.en: basic atomicity
sentences.zh: 基本原子性

sentences.en: REPEATABLE READ
sentences.zh: 可重复读取

sentences.en: basic isolation
sentences.zh: 基本隔离

sentences.en: SERIALIZABLE
sentences.zh: 可串行化

sentences.en: strongest
sentences.zh: 最强

blocks.html_en: Anomaly A1 is “dirty reads” (T1 sees the data T2 wrote before it commits, and indeed T1 might even see it if T2 is rolled back).

sentences.en: Anomaly A1 is “dirty reads” (T1 sees the data T2 wrote before it commits, and indeed T1 might even see it if T2 is rolled back).
sentences.zh: 异常A1是“脏读”（T1看到T2在提交之前写入的数据，实际上，如果T2回滚，T1甚至可能看到它）。

blocks.html_en: Anomaly A2 is “inconsistent reads” (T1 reads a row, T2 does something to it and commits, and T1 reads it again but gets a different value).

sentences.en: Anomaly A2 is “inconsistent reads” (T1 reads a row, T2 does something to it and commits, and T1 reads it again but gets a different value).
sentences.zh: 异常 A2 是“不一致读取”（T1 读取一行，T2 对其执行某些操作并提交，T1 再次读取该行但获得不同的值）。

blocks.html_en: Anomaly A3 is “phantoms” (T1 reads some rows via a select predicate, T2 changes the set of rows matching that predicate, e.g. by adding a new row, and commits, and T1 reads again but this time the select results are slightly different due to the actions of T2, so they still aren’t perfectly isolated).

sentences.en: Anomaly A3 is “phantoms” (T1 reads some rows via a select predicate, T2 changes the set of rows matching that predicate, e.g. by adding a new row, and commits, and T1 reads again but this time the select results are slightly different due to the actions of T2, so they still aren’t perfectly isolated).
sentences.zh: 异常 A3 是“幻影”（T1 通过选择谓词读取一些行，T2 更改与该谓词匹配的行集，例如通过添加新行并提交，T1 再次读取，但这次选择结果由于 T2 的操作而略有不同，因此它们仍然没有完全隔离）。

sentences.en: Theoretical Relation to Locking
sentences.zh: 与锁定的理论关系

blocks.html_en: A bit of an oversimplification, but in traditional theory, these levels were implemented by different locking regimes for phantom prevention, read sets, and write sets, e.g. none, short (only during the operation), or long (hold till the end of the transaction):

sentences.en: A bit of an oversimplification, but in traditional theory, these levels were implemented by different locking regimes for phantom prevention, read sets, and write sets, e.g. none, short (only during the operation), or long (hold till the end of the transaction):
sentences.zh: 有点过于简单化了，但在传统理论中，这些级别是通过不同的锁定机制来实现的，用于幻像预防、读集和写集，例如无、短（仅在操作期间）或长（保持到交易结束）：

sentences.en: Isolation
sentences.zh: 隔离

sentences.en: Phantom Locks
sentences.zh: 幻影锁

sentences.en: Read Locks
sentences.zh: 读锁

sentences.en: Write Locks
sentences.zh: 写锁

sentences.en: READ UNCOMMITTED
sentences.zh: 未提交的阅读

sentences.en: READ COMMITTED
sentences.zh: 已提交读

sentences.en: REPEATABLE READ
sentences.zh: 可重复读取

sentences.en: SERIALIZABLE
sentences.zh: 可串行化

blocks.html_en: Note that the phantom prevention locks can take many forms, e.g. predicate locks, precision locks, gap locks, range locks, index locks, etc, but the basic goal is to make sure the matched set of a predicate does not vary, e.g. between repeated SELECTs in a transaction.

sentences.en: Note that the phantom prevention locks can take many forms, e.g. predicate locks, precision locks, gap locks, range locks, index locks, etc, but the basic goal is to make sure the matched set of a predicate does not vary, e.g. between repeated SELECTs in a transaction.
sentences.zh: 请注意，幻象预防锁可以采取多种形式，例如谓词锁、精度锁、间隙锁、范围锁、索引锁等，但基本目标是确保谓词的匹配集不会变化，例如事务中重复的 SELECT 之间。

sentences.en: But in Reality
sentences.zh: 但在现实中

blocks.html_en: Modern databases don’t necessarily provide exactly these isolation semantics or implement them using the above simple locking regimes.

sentences.en: Modern databases don’t necessarily provide exactly these isolation semantics or implement them using the above simple locking regimes.
sentences.zh: 现代数据库不一定完全提供这些隔离语义或使用上述简单的锁定机制来实现它们。

blocks.html_en: MVCC systems that can read at a specific timestamp are such a convenient tool and intuitive concept, they are widely used to achieve read set isolation and phantom prevention with less locking, higher performance, and higher concurrency. The practice is now quite prevalent.

sentences.en: MVCC systems that can read at a specific timestamp are such a convenient tool and intuitive concept, they are widely used to achieve read set isolation and phantom prevention with less locking, higher performance, and higher concurrency.
sentences.zh: 能够在特定时间戳读取的MVCC系统是如此方便的工具和直观的概念，它们被广泛用于实现读集隔离和幻象预防，同时具有更少的锁定、更高的性能和更高的并发性。

sentences.en: The practice is now quite prevalent.
sentences.zh: 这种做法现在相当普遍。

blocks.html_en: Thus, PostgreSQL uses snapshot isolation internally, even in its default isolation level of Read Committed. MySQL also acquires a read snapshot as of the first select in a transaction, and uses the same snapshot during subsequent selects, in its default isolation level of Repeatable Read.

sentences.en: Thus, PostgreSQL uses snapshot isolation internally, even in its default isolation level of Read Committed.
sentences.zh: 因此，PostgreSQL 在内部使用快照隔离，即使在其默认的已提交读隔离级别也是如此。

sentences.en: MySQL also acquires a read snapshot as of the first select in a transaction, and uses the same snapshot during subsequent selects, in its default isolation level of Repeatable Read.
sentences.zh: MySQL 还会获取事务中第一个选择的读快照，并在后续选择期间使用相同的快照，其默认隔离级别为可重复读。

blocks.html_en: According to A Critique of ANSI SQL Isolation Levels (SIGMOD 1995) by Berenson, Bernstein, Gray et al, the ANSI standard isolation levels suffer from a number of shortcomings, such as failure to address “write skew” anomalies (e.g. the history sequence T1:read[x], T2:read[y], T1:write[y], T2:write[x], T1:commit, T2:commit, which clearly contains a non-serializable overlap between T1 and T2).

sentences.en: According to A Critique of ANSI SQL Isolation Levels (SIGMOD 1995) by Berenson, Bernstein, Gray et al, the ANSI standard isolation levels suffer from a number of shortcomings, such as failure to address “write skew” anomalies (e.g. the history sequence T1:read[x], T2:read[y], T1:write[y], T2:write[x], T1:commit, T2:commit, which clearly contains a non-serializable overlap between T1 and T2).
sentences.zh: 根据 Berenson、Bernstein、Gray 等人对 ANSI SQL 隔离级别的批评 (SIGMOD 1995)，ANSI 标准隔离级别存在许多缺点，例如无法解决“写入倾斜”异常（例如历史序列 T1:read[x]、T2:read[y]、T1:write[y]、T2:write[x]、T1:commit、T2:commit，其中显然包含 T1 和 T2 之间的不可序列化重叠）。

blocks.html_en: The paper Generalized Isolation Level Definitions (ICDE 2000) by Adya, Liskov, et al further elaborates on the theoretical shortcomings of the ANSI standard, and provides more abstract, general, and flexible definitions.

sentences.en: The paper Generalized Isolation Level Definitions (ICDE 2000) by Adya, Liskov, et al further elaborates on the theoretical shortcomings of the ANSI standard, and provides more abstract, general, and flexible definitions.
sentences.zh: Adya、Liskov等人的论文《Generalized Isolation Level Definitions》（ICDE 2000）进一步阐述了ANSI标准的理论缺陷，并提供了更加抽象、通用和灵活的定义。

blocks.html_en: A careful review of the literature shows that Snapshot Isolation (SI) is very similar to Repeatable Read (RR), but they aren’t exactly the same. They are both stronger than Read Committed but weaker than Serializable. The best summary of their difference is that SI allows write skew but blocks phantoms, whereas RR blocks write skew but allows phantoms.

sentences.en: A careful review of the literature shows that Snapshot Isolation (SI) is very similar to Repeatable Read (RR), but they aren’t exactly the same.
sentences.zh: 仔细查阅文献发现，快照隔离（SI）与可重复读（RR）非常相似，但并不完全相同。

sentences.en: They are both stronger than Read Committed but weaker than Serializable.
sentences.zh: 它们都比 Read Comified 强，但比 Serialized 弱。

sentences.en: The best summary of their difference is that SI allows write skew but blocks phantoms, whereas RR blocks write skew but allows phantoms.
sentences.zh: 对它们差异的最好总结是 SI 允许写入偏斜但阻止幻象，而 RR 阻止写入偏斜但允许幻象。

blocks.html_en: Microsoft extends SQL to distinguish between RR and SI.

sentences.en: Microsoft extends SQL to distinguish between RR and SI.
sentences.zh: Microsoft 扩展了 SQL 以区分 RR 和 SI。

blocks.html_en: Infamously, one large database vender (rhymes with Auracle) sold SI to their customers as Serializable.

sentences.en: Infamously, one large database vender (rhymes with Auracle) sold SI to their customers as Serializable.
sentences.zh: 臭名昭著的是，一家大型数据库供应商（与 Auracle 押韵）将 SI 作为 Serialized 出售给他们的客户。

blocks.html_en: Recent versions of PostgreSQL provide an improvement on SI called Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI). For the sake of this document, we will borrow a great example of write skew anomalies from the paper Serializable Snapshot Isolation in PostgreSQL (VLDB 2012) by Dan Ports et al.

sentences.en: Recent versions of PostgreSQL provide an improvement on SI called Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI).
sentences.zh: PostgreSQL 的最新版本对 SI 进行了改进，称为可序列化快照隔离 (SSI)。

sentences.en: For the sake of this document, we will borrow a great example of write skew anomalies from the paper Serializable Snapshot Isolation in PostgreSQL (VLDB 2012) by Dan Ports et al.
sentences.zh: 为了本文档的目的，我们将借用 Dan Ports 等人的论文《PostgreSQL 中的可序列化快照隔离》(VLDB 2012) 中的写入倾斜异常的一个很好的示例。

sentences.en: Let’s Experiment
sentences.zh: 让我们实验一下

sentences.zh: 安装中

sentences.en: As root, quickie unsafe install:
sentences.zh: 以 root 身份快速进行不安全安装：

sentences.en: Connect to the insecure database like so:

sentences.zh: 选择隔离级别

blocks.html_en: InnoDB defaults to RR, but we’ll specify it manually just to be sure.

sentences.en: InnoDB defaults to RR, but we’ll specify it manually just to be sure.
sentences.zh: InnoDB 默认为 RR，但为了确定起见，我们将手动指定它。

sentences.en: Write Skew Test Setup
sentences.zh: 写入偏差测试设置

blocks.html_en: MySQL will likely default to InnoDB, but we’ll specify it just to be sure.

sentences.en: MySQL will likely default to InnoDB, but we’ll specify it just to be sure.
sentences.zh: MySQL 可能默认为 InnoDB，但为了确定起见，我们将指定它。

sentences.en: PostgreSQL
Installing
sentences.zh: 安装中

sentences.en: As root:
sentences.zh: 作为根用户：

sentences.en: Creating a Test Database
sentences.zh: 创建测试数据库

sentences.en: Connecting to Command Line Prompt
sentences.zh: 连接到命令行提示符

sentences.en: Selecting Isolation Level
sentences.zh: 选择隔离级别

sentences.en: Write Skew Test Setup
sentences.zh: 写入偏差测试设置

blocks.html_en: Looks just like with MySQL, except, instead of ENGINE=InnoDB we would say USING someAccessMethod if we wanted to override the default internal table access method.

sentences.en: Looks just like with MySQL, except, instead of ENGINE=InnoDB we would say USING someAccessMethod if we wanted to override the default internal table access method.
sentences.zh: 看起来就像 MySQL，除了，如果我们想覆盖默认的内部表访问方法，我们会说 USING someAccessMethod，而不是 ENGINE=InnoDB。

sentences.en: The Actual Write Skew Test
sentences.zh: 实际写入偏差测试

blocks.html_en: Happily, one version suffices for both MySQL and PostgreSQL.

sentences.en: Happily, one version suffices for both MySQL and PostgreSQL.
sentences.zh: 令人高兴的是，一个版本足以同时支持 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL。

blocks.html_en: Terminal 1: Andy is checking whether it’s safe for him to take some time off:

sentences.en: Terminal 1: Andy is checking whether it’s safe for him to take some time off:
sentences.zh: 1 号航站楼：安迪正在检查他休息一段时间是否安全：

blocks.html_en: Good. There’s another doctor on call. Speaking of which…

sentences.en: Good.
sentences.zh: 好的。

sentences.en: There’s another doctor on call.
sentences.zh: 还有另一位医生值班。

sentences.en: Speaking of which…
sentences.zh: 说到这……

blocks.html_en: Terminal 2: Brad, the other doctor, has the same plan:

sentences.en: Terminal 2: Brad, the other doctor, has the same plan:
sentences.zh: 2 号航站楼：另一位医生布拉德也有同样的计划：

sentences.en: Cool, Brad is all set.
sentences.zh: 太棒了，布拉德已经准备好了。

blocks.html_en: Back at terminal 1: Andy thinks “quick let’s get in before anything changes”:

sentences.en: Back at terminal 1: Andy thinks “quick let’s get in before anything changes”:
sentences.zh: 回到 1 号航站楼：安迪想“在事情发生变化之前我们赶紧进去吧”：

blocks.html_en: So far so good. Andy is a careful boy and takes a moment to double check before he commits:

sentences.en: So far so good.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，一切都很好。

sentences.en: Andy is a careful boy and takes a moment to double check before he commits:
sentences.zh: 安迪是一个细心的男孩，在做出承诺之前会花一些时间仔细检查：

blocks.html_en: The latter SELECT result seems to make sense–roughly speaking RR says we see a consistent view of the world, untainted by changes wrought by any other transaction… a stable read set, except we do see changes wrought by our own transaction (i.e. Andy is no longer on call).

sentences.en: The latter SELECT result seems to make sense–roughly speaking RR says we see a consistent view of the world, untainted by changes wrought by any other transaction…
sentences.zh: 后一个 SELECT 结果似乎是有道理的——粗略地说，RR 表示我们看到了一致的世界观，不受任何其他事务造成的变化的污染……

sentences.en: a stable read set, except we do see changes wrought by our own transaction (i.e. Andy is no longer on call).
sentences.zh: 稳定的读取集，除了我们确实看到我们自己的事务造成的更改（即 Andy 不再待命）。

blocks.html_en: Excellent! All’s well that ends well. Andy’s name is not in the result set, because he’s taking time off, but there is at least one name in the result set. Andy reflects on how awesome SQL is.

sentences.en: Excellent!
sentences.zh: 出色的！

sentences.en: All’s well that ends well.
sentences.zh: 一切都好，结局好。

sentences.en: Andy’s name is not in the result set, because he’s taking time off, but there is at least one name in the result set.
sentences.zh: Andy 的名字不在结果集中，因为他正在休假，但结果集中至少有一个名字。

sentences.en: Andy reflects on how awesome SQL is.
sentences.zh: Andy 反思了 SQL 的神奇之处。

blocks.html_en: However… it didn’t actually end well. Imagine Andy’s surpise when Charlie shows him this:

sentences.en: However…
sentences.zh: 然而…

sentences.en: it didn’t actually end well.
sentences.zh: 实际上结局并不好。

sentences.en: Imagine Andy’s surpise when Charlie shows him this:
sentences.zh: 想象一下，当查理向安迪展示这个时，他会感到多么惊讶：

sentences.en: WHAT!!!!!
How did this happen?
sentences.zh: 这是怎么发生的？

sentences.en: Epilogue
sentences.zh: 结语

blocks.html_en: Andy was bitten by the infamous write skew anomaly, which is not allowed under RR, but is allowed under SI. By the way, this happens in both MySQL RR and PostgreSQL RR.

sentences.en: Andy was bitten by the infamous write skew anomaly, which is not allowed under RR, but is allowed under SI.
sentences.zh: Andy 被臭名昭著的写入倾斜异常所困扰，这种异常在 RR 下是不允许的，但在 SI 下是允许的。

sentences.en: By the way, this happens in both MySQL RR and PostgreSQL RR.
sentences.zh: 顺便说一句，这种情况在 MySQL RR 和 PostgreSQL RR 中都会发生。

blocks.html_en: Simply put, Andy and Brad were operating from the same snapshotted read view, against which their writes were individually valid, but the two transactions overlap in a non-serializable way, and the combination of overlapping writes lead to an unacceptable outcome–there is nobody oncall. Write skew can ruin your day. Phantom protection seems fine in this example though–which is again consistent with SI.

sentences.en: Simply put, Andy and Brad were operating from the same snapshotted read view, against which their writes were individually valid, but the two transactions overlap in a non-serializable way, and the combination of overlapping writes lead to an unacceptable outcome–there is nobody oncall.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，Andy 和 Brad 从相同的快照读取视图进行操作，他们的写入单独有效，但两个事务以不可序列化的方式重叠，并且重叠写入的组合会导致不可接受的结果 - 没有人值班。

sentences.en: Write skew can ruin your day.
sentences.zh: 写入偏差可能会毁了你的一天。

sentences.en: Phantom protection seems fine in this example though–which is again consistent with SI.
sentences.zh: 不过，在这个例子中，幻象保护似乎很好——这又与 SI 一致。

blocks.html_en: What does this tell us about MySQL and PostgreSQL Repeatable Read isolation? It looks technically more like Snapshot Isolation based on these experiments, though it’s probably not purely either one. Perhaps the developers were reticent to depart from the ANSI standard otherwise they would call it Snapshot.

sentences.en: What does this tell us about MySQL and PostgreSQL Repeatable Read isolation?
sentences.zh: 关于 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 可重复读隔离，这告诉我们什么？

sentences.en: It looks technically more like Snapshot Isolation based on these experiments, though it’s probably not purely either one.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，它看起来更像是基于这些实验的快照隔离，尽管它可能不纯粹是其中之一。

sentences.en: Perhaps the developers were reticent to depart from the ANSI standard otherwise they would call it Snapshot.
sentences.zh: 也许开发人员不愿意背离 ANSI 标准，否则他们会将其称为“快照”。

sentences.en: Minor Plot Twist
sentences.zh: 情节小转折

blocks.html_en: It’s not exactly an unexpected plot twist, but it turns out RR in MySQL is weirder than you think. It is not really SI either, if you consider the lost updates anomaly, which should not be allowed in either RR or SI.

sentences.en: It’s not exactly an unexpected plot twist, but it turns out RR in MySQL is weirder than you think.
sentences.zh: 这并不是一个意想不到的情节转折，但事实证明 MySQL 中的 RR 比你想象的更奇怪。

sentences.en: It is not really SI either, if you consider the lost updates anomaly, which should not be allowed in either RR or SI.
sentences.zh: 如果您考虑丢失更新异常，这也不是真正的 SI，这在 RR 或 SI 中都是不允许的。

blocks.html_en: The aforementioned critique of ANSI isolation levels seems convinced that lost updates are not allowed in RR, and notes that “… the anomaly P4 is useful in distinguishing isolation levels intermediate in strength between READ COMMITTED and REPEATABLE READ.” In other words, RC may exhibit lost updates, but RR does not.

sentences.en: The aforementioned critique of ANSI isolation levels seems convinced that lost updates are not allowed in RR, and notes that “…
sentences.zh: 上述对 ANSI 隔离级别的批评似乎确信 RR 中不允许丢失更新，并指出“……

sentences.en: the anomaly P4 is useful in distinguishing isolation levels intermediate in strength between READ COMMITTED and REPEATABLE READ.”
sentences.zh: 异常 P4 对于区分已提交读和可重复读之间强度中等的隔离级别很有用。”

sentences.en: In other words, RC may exhibit lost updates, but RR does not.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，RC 可能会出现更新丢失的情况，但 RR 不会。

blocks.html_en: And yet, according to the following test, in MySQL, lost updates happen even in RR. On the other hand, the same test in PostgreSQL does not exhibit the lost update anomaly in RR, though it does in RC, which conforms better to what the literature says for these isolation levels.

sentences.en: And yet, according to the following test, in MySQL, lost updates happen even in RR.
sentences.zh: 然而，根据下面的测试，在MySQL中，即使在RR中也会发生丢失更新的情况。

sentences.en: On the other hand, the same test in PostgreSQL does not exhibit the lost update anomaly in RR, though it does in RC, which conforms better to what the literature says for these isolation levels.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，PostgreSQL 中的相同测试并没有在 RR 中表现出丢失更新异常，尽管在 RC 中表现出来，这更好地符合文献中对这些隔离级别的描述。

sentences.en: Lost Update Test Setup
sentences.zh: 丢失更新测试设置

blocks.html_en: We just need one row for this…

sentences.en: We just need one row for this…
sentences.zh: 我们只需要一行......

sentences.en: Lost Update Test
sentences.zh: 丢失更新测试

sentences.zh: 1 号航站楼（要求存入 20 美元）：

sentences.en: While this is going on, in terminal 2 (seeking to deposit $30):

sentences.en: Back to terminal 1, which saw a balance of $100:
sentences.zh: 回到 1 号航站楼，余额为 100 美元：

blocks.html_en: This is bad, it means the $30 deposit was overwritten. From the user’s perspective, either the total should be $150, or one of the transactions should have failed. This is a thing that can happen to you in RR isolation level on MySQL.

sentences.en: This is bad, it means the $30 deposit was overwritten.
sentences.zh: 这很糟糕，这意味着 30 美元的押金被覆盖了。

sentences.en: From the user’s perspective, either the total should be $150, or one of the transactions should have failed.
sentences.zh: 从用户的角度来看，要么总额应该为 150 美元，要么其中一笔交易应该失败。

sentences.en: This is a thing that can happen to you in RR isolation level on MySQL.
sentences.zh: 在 MySQL 的 RR 隔离级别中，这种情况可能会发生。

sentences.en: Some Advice re Lost Update
sentences.zh: 关于丢失更新的一些建议

blocks.html_en: Although Serializable isolation level would prevented this problem in MySQL, if you are doing this type of transaction pattern, consider making the database aware of your intent by using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE.

sentences.en: Although Serializable isolation level would prevented this problem in MySQL, if you are doing this type of transaction pattern, consider making the database aware of your intent by using SELECT ...
sentences.zh: 尽管可序列化隔离级别可以防止 MySQL 中出现此问题，但如果您正在执行这种类型的事务模式，请考虑使用 SELECT ... 让数据库了解您的意图。

sentences.en: FOR UPDATE.
sentences.zh: 用于更新。

sentences.en: Summary
sentences.zh: 概括

blocks.html_en: People don’t always define terms in the same way (even when they think they do), and practice often diverges from theory. This is especially so in the world of database isolation levels.

sentences.en: People don’t always define terms in the same way (even when they think they do), and practice often diverges from theory.
sentences.zh: 人们并不总是以相同的方式定义术语（即使他们认为是这样），而且实践常常与理论不同。

sentences.en: This is especially so in the world of database isolation levels.
sentences.zh: 在数据库隔离级别领域尤其如此。

blocks.html_en: The article Comprehensive Understanding of Transaction Isolation Levels has more details comparing the nuances of some popular implementations.

sentences.en: The article Comprehensive Understanding of Transaction Isolation Levels has more details comparing the nuances of some popular implementations.
sentences.zh: 全面理解事务隔离级别一文提供了更多详细信息，比较了一些流行实现的细微差别。

content_html_zh:
可重复读取与快照隔离

这里有一个问题：当你在 MySQL 中选择可重复读隔离级别时，它实际上会给你快照隔离语义吗？ 它们有何不同？为什么如此重要？

理论

ANSI 标准隔离级别

PostgreSQL、MySQL、SAP 和许多其他数据库基于异常保护实现了 SQL-92 中定义的四种常见隔离级别：

隔离 概括 A1 A2 A3
未提交的阅读 最弱的 Yes Yes Yes
已提交读 基本原子性 no Yes Yes
可重复读取 基本隔离 no no Yes
可串行化 最强 no no no

异常A1是“脏读”（T1看到T2在提交之前写入的数据，实际上，如果T2回滚，T1甚至可能看到它）。

异常 A2 是“不一致读取”（T1 读取一行，T2 对其执行某些操作并提交，T1 再次读取该行但获得不同的值）。

异常 A3 是“幻影”（T1 通过选择谓词读取一些行，T2 更改与该谓词匹配的行集，例如通过添加新行并提交，T1 再次读取，但这次选择结果由于 T2 的操作而略有不同，因此它们仍然没有完全隔离）。

与锁定的理论关系

有点过于简单化了，但在传统理论中，这些级别是通过不同的锁定机制来实现的，用于幻像预防、读集和写集，例如无、短（仅在操作期间）或长（保持到交易结束）：

隔离 幻影锁 读锁 写锁
未提交的阅读 no no long
已提交读 no short long
可重复读取 short long long
可串行化 long long long

请注意，幻象预防锁可以采取多种形式，例如谓词锁、精度锁、间隙锁、范围锁、索引锁等，但基本目标是确保谓词的匹配集不会变化，例如事务中重复的 SELECT 之间。

但在现实中

现代数据库不一定完全提供这些隔离语义或使用上述简单的锁定机制来实现它们。

能够在特定时间戳读取的MVCC系统是如此方便的工具和直观的概念，它们被广泛用于实现读集隔离和幻象预防，同时具有更少的锁定、更高的性能和更高的并发性。 这种做法现在相当普遍。

因此，PostgreSQL 在内部使用快照隔离，即使在其默认的已提交读隔离级别也是如此。 MySQL 还会获取事务中第一个选择的读快照，并在后续选择期间使用相同的快照，其默认隔离级别为可重复读。

根据 Berenson、Bernstein、Gray 等人对 ANSI SQL 隔离级别的批评 (SIGMOD 1995)，ANSI 标准隔离级别存在许多缺点，例如无法解决“写入倾斜”异常（例如历史序列 T1:read[x]、T2:read[y]、T1:write[y]、T2:write[x]、T1:commit、T2:commit，其中显然包含 T1 和 T2 之间的不可序列化重叠）。

Adya、Liskov等人的论文《Generalized Isolation Level Definitions》（ICDE 2000）进一步阐述了ANSI标准的理论缺陷，并提供了更加抽象、通用和灵活的定义。

仔细查阅文献发现，快照隔离（SI）与可重复读（RR）非常相似，但并不完全相同。 它们都比 Read Comified 强，但比 Serialized 弱。 对它们差异的最好总结是 SI 允许写入偏斜但阻止幻象，而 RR 阻止写入偏斜但允许幻象。

Microsoft 扩展了 SQL 以区分 RR 和 SI。

臭名昭著的是，一家大型数据库供应商（与 Auracle 押韵）将 SI 作为 Serialized 出售给他们的客户。

PostgreSQL 的最新版本对 SI 进行了改进，称为可序列化快照隔离 (SSI)。 为了本文档的目的，我们将借用 Dan Ports 等人的论文《PostgreSQL 中的可序列化快照隔离》(VLDB 2012) 中的写入倾斜异常的一个很好的示例。

让我们实验一下

MySQL

安装中

以 root 身份快速进行不安全安装：

# dnf install -y community-mysql-server
# systemctl start mysqld

创建测试数据库

# mysql -u root -e 'CREATE DATABASE test'

连接到命令行提示符

像这样连接到不安全的数据库：

# mysql -u root -D test

选择隔离级别

InnoDB 默认为 RR，但为了确定起见，我们将手动指定它。

mysql> SET SESSION TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;

写入偏差测试设置

MySQL 可能默认为 InnoDB，但为了确定起见，我们将指定它。

CREATE TABLE doctor (name VARCHAR(20), oncall BOOLEAN) ENGINE=InnoDB;
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Andy', TRUE);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Brad', TRUE);
EXIT

PostgreSQL

安装中

作为根用户：

# dnf install -y https://download.postgresql.org/pub/repos/yum/reporpms/F-37-x86_64/pgdg-fedora-repo-latest.noarch.rpm
# dnf install -y postgresql14-server postgresql14
# /usr/pgsql-14/bin/postgresql-14-setup initdb
# systemctl start postgresql-14

创建测试数据库

# su -c 'createdb test' - postgres

连接到命令行提示符

# su -c 'psql -d test' - postgres

选择隔离级别

test=# SET SESSION CHARACTERISTICS AS TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL REPEATABLE READ;

写入偏差测试设置

看起来就像 MySQL，除了，如果我们想覆盖默认的内部表访问方法，我们会说 USING someAccessMethod，而不是 ENGINE=InnoDB。

CREATE TABLE doctor (name VARCHAR(20), oncall BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Andy', TRUE);
INSERT INTO doctor (name, oncall) VALUES ('Brad', TRUE);
EXIT

实际写入偏差测试

令人高兴的是，一个版本足以同时支持 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL。

1 号航站楼：安迪正在检查他休息一段时间是否安全：

BEGIN;
SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE AND name<>'Andy';
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Brad |
+------+

好的。 还有另一位医生值班。 说到这……

2 号航站楼：另一位医生布拉德也有同样的计划：

BEGIN;
SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE AND name<>'Brad';
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Andy |
+------+

UPDATE doctor SET oncall=FALSE WHERE name='Brad';
COMMIT;

太棒了，布拉德已经准备好了。

回到 1 号航站楼：安迪想“在事情发生变化之前我们赶紧进去吧”：

UPDATE doctor SET oncall=FALSE WHERE name='Andy';

到目前为止，一切都很好。 安迪是一个细心的男孩，在做出承诺之前会花一些时间仔细检查：

SELECT name FROM doctor WHERE oncall=TRUE;
+------+
| name |
+------+
| Brad |
+------+

COMMIT;

后一个 SELECT 结果似乎是有道理的——粗略地说，RR 表示我们看到了一致的世界观，不受任何其他事务造成的变化的污染…… 稳定的读取集，除了我们确实看到我们自己的事务造成的更改（即 Andy 不再待命）。

出色的！ 一切都好，结局好。 Andy 的名字不在结果集中，因为他正在休假，但结果集中至少有一个名字。 Andy 反思了 SQL 的神奇之处。

然而… 实际上结局并不好。 想象一下，当查理向安迪展示这个时，他会感到多么惊讶：

SELECT * FROM doctor;
+------+--------+
| name | oncall |
+------+--------+
| Brad |      0 |
| Andy |      0 |
+------+--------+

什么！！！！！ 这是怎么发生的？

结语

Andy 被臭名昭著的写入倾斜异常所困扰，这种异常在 RR 下是不允许的，但在 SI 下是允许的。 顺便说一句，这种情况在 MySQL RR 和 PostgreSQL RR 中都会发生。

简而言之，Andy 和 Brad 从相同的快照读取视图进行操作，他们的写入单独有效，但两个事务以不可序列化的方式重叠，并且重叠写入的组合会导致不可接受的结果 - 没有人值班。 写入偏差可能会毁了你的一天。 不过，在这个例子中，幻象保护似乎很好——这又与 SI 一致。

关于 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL 可重复读隔离，这告诉我们什么？ 从技术上讲，它看起来更像是基于这些实验的快照隔离，尽管它可能不纯粹是其中之一。 也许开发人员不愿意背离 ANSI 标准，否则他们会将其称为“快照”。

情节小转折

这并不是一个意想不到的情节转折，但事实证明 MySQL 中的 RR 比你想象的更奇怪。 如果您考虑丢失更新异常，这也不是真正的 SI，这在 RR 或 SI 中都是不允许的。

上述对 ANSI 隔离级别的批评似乎确信 RR 中不允许丢失更新，并指出“…… 异常 P4 对于区分已提交读和可重复读之间强度中等的隔离级别很有用。” 换句话说，RC 可能会出现更新丢失的情况，但 RR 不会。

然而，根据下面的测试，在MySQL中，即使在RR中也会发生丢失更新的情况。 另一方面，PostgreSQL 中的相同测试并没有在 RR 中表现出丢失更新异常，尽管在 RC 中表现出来，这更好地符合文献中对这些隔离级别的描述。

丢失更新测试设置

我们只需要一行......

CREATE TABLE account (id INT, cash INT);
INSERT INTO account (id, cash) VALUES (1, 100);

丢失更新测试

1 号航站楼（要求存入 20 美元）：

BEGIN;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  100 |
+------+

在此过程中，在 2 号航站楼（要求存入 30 美元）：

BEGIN;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  100 |
+------+

UPDATE account SET cash=130 WHERE id=1;
COMMIT;

回到 1 号航站楼，余额为 100 美元：

UPDATE account SET cash=120 WHERE id=1;
COMMIT;
SELECT cash FROM account WHERE id=1;
+------+
| cash |
+------+
|  120 |
+------+

这很糟糕，这意味着 30 美元的押金被覆盖了。 从用户的角度来看，要么总额应该为 150 美元，要么其中一笔交易应该失败。 在 MySQL 的 RR 隔离级别中，这种情况可能会发生。

关于丢失更新的一些建议

尽管可序列化隔离级别可以防止 MySQL 中出现此问题，但如果您正在执行这种类型的事务模式，请考虑使用 SELECT ... 让数据库了解您的意图。 用于更新。

概括

人们并不总是以相同的方式定义术语（即使他们认为是这样），而且实践常常与理论不同。 在数据库隔离级别领域尤其如此。

全面理解事务隔离级别一文提供了更多详细信息，比较了一些流行实现的细微差别。
